```md
  behavior, and SQLite CAS `UPDATE ... RETURNING` all point to a future
  "inserted/claimed/conflicted" result abstraction.
- **Statement assembly is the key package-boundary decision.** The examples can
  render advanced SQL today, but ETL/analytics packages need a stable assembler
  or core compile target that does not depend on ORM internals.

### Post-implementation status (2026-07-02)

A review of the two advanced-SQL example families against these contracts, after
waves 0ŌĆō4, confirmed (by rebuilding each `"raw"` case with the shipped
primitives and rendering it) that the graduation's fix targets are **now
builder-native** for contracts **01ŌĆō07** and the **index half of 11**:

| Contract              | Fix target                          | Now provided by                                                               |
| --------------------- | ----------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 01 ETL rollup         | expression aliases                  | `expr()` (item 7) + assembly seam (5/13)                                      |
| 02 Window analytics   | `over()`/frames/ranking             | `over`/`WindowSpec`/`rank`/ŌĆ” (item 10)                                        |
| 03 Sessionization     | `lag`/date-diff                     | `lag` + `dateDiff` (item 10), SQLite-verified                                 |
| 04 Top-N per group    | `row_number` + derived-table seam   | `rowNumber` + `over` + `.as()` (item 10)                                      |
| 05 Cohort retention   | date-bucket/date-diff + alias reuse | `dateTrunc`/`dateDiff`/`expr` (9/10)                                          |
| 06 Funnel analysis    | first-event helpers / alias reuse   | `filter(min(...))` + `dateAdd` + `expr`                                       |
| 07 Recursive comments | `WITH RECURSIVE` builder            | `$withRecursive` (item 11)                                                    |
| 11 (index half)       | expression + partial indexes        | `index().on(sql\`ŌĆ”\`).where(ŌĆ”)` ŌĆö snapshot + pg/sqlite DDL; mysql fail-closes |

**After wave 5**, the remaining v0.8 tracking items are closed too: contract
**10** (JSON set-returning) ŌåÆ item 14; the **generated-columns half of 11** ŌåÆ
item 15; and the **ODKU assignment-order** hazard (contract 12 pressure) ŌåÆ item
19. Contracts **08** (portable lock/claim) and **09** (checkpoint contract)
remain routed to **v0.9**. The example families still carry `"raw"`
implementations and stale `v08PainPoint` annotations for the now-solved cases;
refreshing them to builder-native is logged in
[v0.9](v0.9.0-roadmap.md#required-examples--tests).

## Open questions

- ~~**Compose-only fragment IR, or add an introspectable expression AST?**~~
  **Decided (wave 1, 2026-07-02): defer the AST, ship the seam** ŌĆö the fragment
  IR stays the compile target, and the additive extension point is **shipped**:
  an opaque `meta` slot on `SqlChunk`
  (`SqlChunkMeta`/`withSqlChunkMeta`/`sqlChunkMeta`), renderer-ignored and
  carried by reference through composition, proven by the round-trip fixture
  `packages/orm/sql_meta_seam_test.ts` (annotations survive the `sql` tag and
  builder assembly with byte-identical render output). A transformable AST can
  populate the slot later as a non-breaking version bump ŌĆö v0.13 DuckDB pushdown
  is unblocked either way (audit Fix 3 satisfied).
- ~~Expose the statement assembler publicly, or require downstream packages to
  compile fragments + a thin assembly helper?~~ **Decided (wave 1, 2026-07-02):
  expose a minimal assembly API from `@sisal/core`** ŌĆö deterministic
  assemble-from-parts functions (select / insert-from-select with group-by and
  upsert), **not** the fluent OLTP builder, which stays in `@sisal/orm`.
  Rationale: fragments-only would force ETL/analytics to hand-assemble whole
  statements (the "raw SQL is too necessary" pain point), while exposing the
  fluent builder would drag the OLTP surface into the compile target. The G1
  dependency direction holds: `@sisal/etl` / `@sisal/analytics` depend on core
  only. The wave-4 core-only fixture (item 13) may use exactly this API, nothing
  more.
- ~~Does `@sisal/migrate` depend on `@sisal/core` only, or still on
  `@sisal/orm`?~~ **Decided (wave 1, 2026-07-02): core only.** Everything
  `@sisal/migrate` consumes (the snapshot contract in `schema.ts`, errors, the
  render primitives) lives in the extracted lower tier; no OLTP-builder
  dependency exists to keep. The wave-2 extraction re-points it, and the
  import-migration notes record the change. Coupled decision, same day:
  **checkpoint-table ownership stays `@sisal/etl`-managed**
  (`sisal_etl_checkpoints`, the v0.6 A3 default, ratified per audit Fix 2) ŌĆö no
  `etl ŌåÆ migrate` edge is added, `architecture.md`'s graph is already correct,
  and v0.9 validates the contract per engine (item 18 closed).

## Acceptance criteria

- `@sisal/core` exists, is published to **JSR** (npm deferred ŌĆö see the
  [npm-release readiness report](npm-release-readiness.md)), and
  `@sisal/orm`/adapters import from it; the public ORM API is unchanged for
  users (`@sisal/orm/core` stays as a compatibility re-export of `@sisal/core`).
- Every IR construct (select/CTE/insert-from-select/aggregate/window where
  supported) has golden per-dialect SQL tests and correct parameter binding.
- A downstream package can construct and render a grouped insert-from-select +
  filtered aggregate using only `@sisal/core` exports (proven by a fixture).
- The transformable-AST decision is documented (build / defer / reject) **and,
  if deferred, the additive extension point is shipped and proven** by a fixture
  that round-trips a fragment through the seam ŌĆö so v0.13 cannot be blocked by a
  frozen IR.
- The capability descriptor's key is **`(engine, variant, version-range)`** ŌĆö
  the v0.7 `DialectIdentity` `(engine, variant, version)` axis generalized with
  version-range predicates. It must express all six matrix/integration
  capability targets (`pg`, `neon`, `sqlite`, `libsql`, `mysql`, `mariadb` ŌĆö
  neon ŌēĀ pg and libsql ŌēĀ sqlite as well as the MySQL/MariaDB variant split), and
  `dialectGuard` is generalized to a version-aware capability check (e.g. MySQL
  Ōēź 8.0.21 for `RETURNING`, Postgres Ōēź 14 for `CYCLE`, SQLite Ōēź 3.31). It is the
  single source the matrix, `dialectGuard`, and ETL/analytics read.
- The **statement-assembly seam decision is closed** ŌĆö exposed minimal assembly
  API or fragments-only ŌĆö documented, and reflected in the core-only fixture
  (which may use exactly what the decision makes public, nothing more).
- The **`@sisal/migrate` dependency decision is closed** (core-only vs
  `@sisal/orm`) and reflected in the extraction's import graph and the migration
  notes.

## Affected packages

New `@sisal/core`; `@sisal/orm`, `@sisal/migrate`, `@sisal/pg`, `@sisal/neon`,
`@sisal/sqlite`, `@sisal/libsql`, `@sisal/mysql` (re-point imports). No
app-visible API break.

## Required examples / tests

- Golden-SQL suites per dialect for all IR constructs.
- A `@sisal/core`-only fixture compiling the v0.6 `post_hourly_stats` rollup
  SQL.
- Prepared-plan round-trip tests.

## Dialect compatibility notes

Capability gates become **declarative** (an `(engine, variant, version-range)`
capability descriptor ŌĆö keyed to express the six matrix targets, **not** the
4-value render dialect, which still collapses neonŌåÆpostgres and libsqlŌåÆsqlite)
rather than scattered render branches. It is the single source the v0.9 matrix
and the `dialectGuard` both read. Window/data-modifying-CTE/insert-from-select
rendering is Postgres-first with explicit gates for the SQLite and MySQL
families.
```

### docs/v0.9.0-roadmap.md

URL: https://gilvandovieira.github.io/sisal/v0.9.0-roadmap.html

```md
---
title: v0.9.0 Roadmap
---

# Sisal v0.9.0 roadmap ŌĆö Adapter Hardening & Capability Matrix

With the IR stabilized in [v0.8](v0.8.0-roadmap.md) and **MySQL added in
[v0.7](v0.7.0-roadmap.md)**, v0.9 hardens the **five** adapters against the
ETL/analytics SQL shapes surfaced by
[v0.6](v0.6.0-roadmap.md)/[v0.7](v0.7.0-roadmap.md), and turns the implicit,
scattered dialect knowledge into an **explicit, tested capability surface**.
This is the last hardening step before the public `@sisal/etl` (v0.10) and
`@sisal/analytics` (v0.11) packages are built on top.

## Status legend

| Symbol | Meaning                                                    |
| ------ | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| ­¤ö┤     | Not started ┬Ę ­¤¤Ī In progress ┬Ę ­¤¤ó Done ŌĆö move to CHANGELOG |

## Goal

Make each adapter a known quantity for ETL/analytics: every dialect-divergent
behavior is **declared, documented, and tested**, with explicit fallbacks where
an engine can't match Postgres.

## Scope / focus

- **PostgreSQL** ŌĆö the strongest ETL/analytics target (insert-from-select,
  FILTER, windows, advisory locks, materialized views); the reference adapter.
- **Neon** ŌĆö Postgres-compatible but **serverless-aware** (WebSocket Pool;
  prefer generated SQL / atomic statements). Separate generic-Postgres behavior
  from Neon-specific behavior in code and docs.
- **SQLite** ŌĆö embedded/local compatibility and fallbacks (`FILTER`ŌåÆ`CASE WHEN`,
  no materialized views, lock-row instead of advisory locks).
- **libSQL** ŌĆö SQLite semantics + **remote/Turso runtime caveats** (hrana/HTTP,
  `ArrayBuffer` BLOBs, `intMode`).
- **MySQL / MariaDB** ŌĆö harden the v0.7 adapter: `ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE`
  upsert, uneven `RETURNING`, `BOOLEAN`=`TINYINT(1)`, `GET_LOCK` named locks,
  and the MySQL-vs-MariaDB split.
- **Transaction behavior differences**; **returning/upsert differences**;
  **timestamp/date function differences** (the v0.6 bucket abstraction per
  dialect); **lock/checkpoint strategy differences** (advisory lock vs lock
  row).
- **Test coverage for dialect-specific fallbacks** ŌĆö every degraded path
  (FILTERŌåÆCASE, ilikeŌåÆLIKE, bucket math) gets a per-engine integration test.

## Specific hardening items (from the audit)

- **Declarative `(engine, variant, version-range)` capability descriptor** (the
  v0.8 surface): one source of truth the feature matrix, the `dialectGuard`, and
  ETL/analytics read. It must distinguish **neon ŌēĀ pg, libsql ŌēĀ sqlite, and
  mysql ŌēĀ mariadb ŌēĀ by-version** ŌĆö the 4-value render dialect cannot (it
  collapses neonŌåÆpg, libsqlŌåÆsqlite), so the matrix's six capability targets plus
  a version axis are authoritative.
- **CTE per-engine execution tests** ŌĆö CTEs render dialect-agnostically today
  but have **no per-engine integration test**; add them for
  pg/neon/sqlite/libsql/mysql/mariadb.
- **Recursive CTE** per-engine verification ŌĆö the builder itself is **v0.8
  work** (its task list, item 11); v0.9 pins the per-engine/per-version behavior
  it renders (MySQL 8+ vs 5.7, MariaDB, SQLite depth/cycle limits), consistent
  with this release's "no new SQL constructs beyond v0.8" non-goal.
- **MySQL functional (expression) indexes ŌĆö version-gate, don't fail-closed
  always** (raised by the v0.8 advanced-SQL review, contract 11). The
  `@sisal/mysql` DDL generator currently rejects **every** expression index
  unconditionally ("version-less generator fails closed"), but MySQL **Ōēź
  8.0.13** supports functional indexes; light them through the capability
  descriptor on MySQL 8.0.13+ and keep the typed rejection only below that and
  on **MariaDB** (which has none). Partial indexes stay `ŌØī` for the whole MySQL
  family. Pin with DDL render tests + a live per-engine check.
- **`@sisal/pg` float8 ŌåÆ number** (v0.5 item 11) ŌĆö the only adapter returning
  `double precision` as a string; fix so numeric reads match the other three
  adapters (critical for analytics math).
- **Insert-from-select / data-modifying CTE** execution verified per engine
  (Postgres yes; SQLite "less fully" ŌĆö pin the exact supported subset).
- **Advisory-lock equivalence** ŌĆö implement and test the v0.6 A2 contract the
  ETL runner will use: `pg_try_advisory_lock` / `pg_advisory_unlock` on
  PostgreSQL-family sessions, `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` for supported SQLite/libSQL
  runs, and `GET_LOCK` / `RELEASE_LOCK` for MySQL-family adapters.
- **Checkpoint-table ownership validation** ŌĆö consume the v0.6 A3 default that
  checkpoints are `@sisal/etl`-managed (`sisal_etl_checkpoints`) rather than an
  `@sisal/migrate` system table. If v0.9 reverses that default, record the
  reason and the resulting `etl ŌåÆ migrate` edge in
  [architecture](architecture.md).
- **Replay-vs-retention refusal** ŌĆö implement and test the v0.6 A4 invariant:
  the per-job `pruned_before` replay horizon advances atomically with the prune
  (never lagging the delete), and `replay`/`backfill` refuse windows behind it
  with a typed error. Test both directions: a refused replay leaves the rollup
  row unchanged (no zero-overwrite), and a crash between prune and horizon
  advance leaves the horizon ahead or equal ŌĆö never behind.

## Deliverables

- A per-dialect **capability descriptor** wired into the generated feature
  matrix.
- Integration tests for every ETL/analytics-relevant shape on every supporting
  adapter (extending the existing gated `integration/` suites).
- Updated `docs/{pg,neon,sqlite,libsql,mysql}-compatibility.md` + the unified
  matrix.

## Non-goals

No new ETL/analytics package; no new SQL constructs beyond what v0.8 stabilized;
no DuckDB.

## Risks

- **Combinatorial test growth** ŌĆö every shape ├Ś six capability targets ├Ś
  fallbacks. Lean on the existing matrix coverage-guard to keep claims honest.
- **Serverless flakiness** ŌĆö Neon WebSocket tests need the `wsproxy` image; keep
  them gated and deterministic.
- **Hidden runtime type lies** (like float8) elsewhere ŌĆö audit numeric/date
  round-trips broadly, not just the known case.

## Open questions

- Recursive CTEs: in scope for analytics (hierarchies) or deferred?
- **Recursive CTE cycle detection** (raised by the v0.8 review, contract 07):
  the shipped `$withRecursive` relies on the portable depth-guard pattern
  (`WHERE depth < n`). Ship the PostgreSQL 14+ `CYCLE`/`SEARCH` clauses as a
  version-gated construct, or keep the depth-guard-only surface? A new construct
  is beyond this release's non-goal, so this is a v0.10+/backlog flag, not v0.9
  build work.
- Is materialized-view DDL worth adding for Postgres (rollup acceleration),
  given SQLite has none?
- How much SQLite-family ETL is "supported" vs "Postgres-first, documented gap"?

## Acceptance criteria

- Every dialect-divergent behavior relevant to ETL/analytics is a declared
  capability with a backing per-engine test (matrix `Ō£ģ`/`ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ`/`ŌØī` all
  test-backed). The descriptor is keyed `(engine, variant, version-range)` (the
  v0.7 `DialectIdentity` axis generalized) and distinguishes all six capability
  targets ŌĆö neon/pg, libsql/sqlite, and mysql/mariadb.
- `@sisal/pg` returns `double precision` as a JS `number`.
- Insert-from-select, upsert-from-select, data-modifying CTEs, filtered
  aggregates, and date buckets each have a per-engine pass/fallback test.
- **The ETL runner's substrate is real, not just designed:** the portable
  claim/advisory-lock abstraction (`pg_try_advisory_lock` / `GET_LOCK` /
  `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` or lock-row) and the checkpoint-table contract each have a
  per-engine test on every engine where ETL is supported (and a documented gap
  where it is not), so v0.10 does not ship a runner on an unbuilt
  lock/checkpoint model. (Promoted from a "hardening item" ŌĆö it is a v0.10
  prerequisite; see the [sequencing audit](roadmap-sequencing-audit.md).)
- **Checkpoint-table ownership is validated and recorded**: v0.9 consumes the
  v0.6 default (`@sisal/etl`-managed `sisal_etl_checkpoints`) or explicitly
  reverses it with the resulting dependency edge reflected in `architecture.md`,
  so v0.10 consumes a settled boundary ŌĆö not an open question restated at ship
  time.
- The five compatibility docs and the six-column unified matrix reflect the
  hardened reality.

## Affected packages

`@sisal/pg`, `@sisal/neon`, `@sisal/sqlite`, `@sisal/libsql`, `@sisal/mysql`,
`@sisal/core` (capability descriptors), `tools/feature_matrix.ts` + docs.

## Required examples / tests

- Per-engine integration tests for each ETL/analytics shape and fallback.
- A numeric round-trip suite (float/numeric/bigint) across all five adapter
  packages and six capability targets.
- **Refresh the advanced-SQL example families to builder-native** (raised by the
  v0.8 review). `examples/{postgres,mysql,sqlite}-family-advanced-sql` still
  implement contracts **01ŌĆō07** and the index-half of **11** as `"raw"` /
  `"hybrid"` with stale `v08PainPoint` annotations that claim missing primitives
  ŌĆö but v0.8 shipped them (`over`/windows, `lag`/`dateDiff`, `rowNumber`,
  `$withRecursive`, `expr` alias reuse, expression/partial indexes). Upgrade
  those cases to the typed primitives, correct the annotations, and keep only
  the genuinely-dialect-native/deferred cases (JSON-table = v0.8 item 14,
  generated columns = v0.8 item 15, lock/claim = contract 08, checkpoint =
  contract 09) as raw with accurate notes.

## Dialect compatibility notes

This release _is_ the dialect compatibility note: it makes the capability matrix
authoritative and machine-checked. PostgreSQL is the reference; Neon mirrors it
with serverless caveats; SQLite/libSQL expose only the subset they support
correctly, with every degradation tested and documented. MySQL/MariaDB share the
`@sisal/mysql` package but keep separate capability rows for named-lock
behavior, the uneven `RETURNING` surface, and MySQL-family typed gaps.
```
